text: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71 - NPR
fullText: His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.

text: Seahawks sale: Answers to five key questions about what’s next - The Seattle Times
fullText: We know who will likely be the next owners of the Seahawks, answering a question that has lingered for months. Here are the answers to five questions about what's next.

text: FIFA finds "no evidence" that ball hit wire in key moment of England vs. Norway quarterfinal - NBC Sports
fullText: Strange moment sparks memories of Will Reichard's field-goal try in England.

text: British police make new arrest in Ann Widdecombe murder investigation - Al Jazeera
fullText: Police arrest 28-year-old suspect in South Yorkshire in connection with the murder of former MP Ann Widdecombe.

text: A Trump Account could make your kid a millionaire—but financial experts warn of a catch - Fortune
fullText: Four financial planners break down how much your kid could really make, where it fits with your 401(k), and the catch parents keep missing.

text: Platner aide hit with brutal timeline check after campaign denied rape claim, then folded days later - Fox News
fullText: Graham Platner suspended his Maine Senate campaign on Wednesday, after Jenny Racicot alleged he raped her.

text: James Murdoch may have reaped as much as $7.5 billion from his pre-IPO investment in Elon Musk’s SpaceX - Fortune
fullText: His early bet on the rocket company has likely made him much richer than his father Rupert Murdoch ever did.

text: Popular NYC museum tests positive for Legionnaires' disease bacteria amid outbreak - Fox News
fullText: The Guggenheim Museum was among 31 Upper East Side buildings ordered to disinfect cooling towers after testing positive for Legionella bacteria.

text: Mexico-US relations are already strained, but experts say they’re about to get worse - CNN
fullText: The death of a Mexican man in Houston at the hands of Immigration and Customs Enforcement is threatening to upend already-strained relations between Mexico and the United States.

text: Taylor Swift Is Officially an Aunt to Travis Kelce’s Nieces—and She’s Ready for It - E! News
fullText: When Taylor Swift wed Travis Kelce at MSG on July 3, she officially became an aunt to his nieces Wyatt, Elliotte, Bennett and Finnley—Jason Kelce and Kylie Kelce's daughters. Read about their bond.

text: Heat wave could threaten all-time record highs in Rockies - The Weather Channel
fullText: All-time records could be set in Utah, Wyoming and Montana, then more heat and humidity will bake the Midwest and East into the week ahead.

text: Volkswagen CEO looks to avoid plant closures as automaker moves to cut costs - AP News
fullText: Volkswagen’s CEO has indicated that he’s trying to avoid closing plants as he seeks to turn around the automaker’s performance. The Wolfsburg, Germany-based company faces pressure to cut costs at home and increasingly intense competition in the lucrative Chin…

text: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, US launches fresh strikes - dw.com
fullText: The Iranian navy has said it closed the Strait of Hormuz after firing on a vessel transiting an "unapproved route." In response, the US launched a third set of strikes on Iran this week. Follow DW for more.

text: Qatar's former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at age 74, state news agency says - AP News
fullText: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at age 74. The state-run Qatar News Agency reported his death without offering a cause. As ruler of Qatar, he transformed the tiny Persian Gulf nation into a global player in diplomacy, media and investment and then …

text: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz; Tehran lashes out again at Gulf Arab states - AP News
fullText: The United States has attacked Iran over an Iranian strike on a vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. The strike early Sunday set a container ship ablaze and forced its crew to abandon it. Iran apparently responded with strikes targeting Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and …

text: At least two killed in Toronto street festival shooting - BBC
fullText: Police say there was an exchange of gunfire between two individuals at the festival, and two firearms had been recovered from the scene.

text: After leader’s funeral, an emboldened Iran tests Trump, risking renewed war - The Washington Post
fullText: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said the Strait of Hormuz would be closed until further notice, accusing the United States of interfering in the waterway.

text: 200 young campers rescued as flooding hits parts of Missouri and Kentucky - NPR
fullText: A historic rainfall event has left communities across several Missouri counties underwater and prompted water rescues, including an evacuation of a summer camp.
